description_long: This monastery features a central rectangular building with a prominent orange-tiled roof and several adjoining wings, all set on a flat, tan base that suggests a courtyard or open grounds. The main structure is surrounded by lower annexes, and a winding path leads up to the entrance, indicating a welcoming approach. The rooflines are simple and gabled, with no visible towers or spires, and the overall design is compact and symmetrical. The color palette is dominated by warm orange and neutral beige tones, giving the building a Mediterranean or Southern European appearance. There are no visible religious symbols or ornate decorations, and the windows are rectangular rather than arched. The structure appears solid and robust, likely constructed from stone or concrete with clay roof tiles.
description: A compact, orange-roofed monastery with a central building and adjoining wings, set on a tan base with a winding path. The design is simple, symmetrical, and Mediterranean in style.
description_view_0: The front view shows the main entrance with the winding path leading up to the central building and the orange-tiled roof.
description_view_1: This side view highlights the symmetrical wings and the flat tan base, with the rooflines clearly visible.
description_view_2: The rear view reveals the back annexes and the continuation of the orange-tiled roof, with the path extending away from the building.
description_view_3: This angled view provides a clear look at the overall layout, including the central structure, wings, and the approach path.
